column1: Data Loss: Campaign brief is cleared when switching tabs during creation
column29:
When a user is in the process of creating a new campaign and fills out the "Brief" section, switching to a different tab (browser or application tab) causes the unsaved brief data to be lost. The data does not persist, forcing the user to re-enter all information.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Navigate to the *Campaign Creation* page.
# Begin entering information into the *Brief* section.
# Without clicking "Save" or "Submit," switch to a different browser tab or application window.
# Switch back to the Campaign Creation tab.
# Observe that the brief content is cleared/missing.

----

h3. *Expected Result*

The content entered in the Brief section should persist if the user navigates away or switches tabs, OR the system should prompt the user to save changes before leaving the page.

----

h3. *Actual Result*

All data entered into the Brief section is cleared, resulting in potential loss of work for the user.
column332:
2026年03月13日 07:57;557058:3a7d9a2e-d321-4b2a-8004-2783dc21035c;*Root Cause:* The {{form.reset()}} was being called every time the campaign data changed (including tab switches that triggered TanStack Query refetches). This overwrote user's typed input with the original server value.

*Fix Applied:* Added an {{initialized}} ref to ensure {{form.reset()}} only runs once on initial load. Subsequent refetches (triggered by tab switches) no longer reset the form, preserving user's unsaved changes.

*Code Change:*{{apps/dashboard/src/app/(v1)/(dashboard)/campaigns/[id]/edit/brief/page.tsx}} (lines 88-99)

column1: [CCID] Admin approve/reject content draft
column29:
Context:

* Source: CCID Current Workflow whiteboard (Content Draft Approval System)
* Whiteboard URL: [https://c-channel.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/LS/whiteboard/2862710803|https://c-channel.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/LS/whiteboard/2862710803]
* Node ID: 4Moww5I5Lyz3mOFx39zGJ ("1. approve/reject content draft")
* This step comes after "Admin check content draft" and before "KOL submit post" in the draft flow.

User story:

As an admin (CS/client), I want to approve or reject content drafts with clear reasons so that KOLs know exactly what to fix and we keep a history of all approval decisions.

Details:

* Admin can be CS, client, or internal final approver.
* Drafts are created in the previously defined step "KOL submit/resubmit content draft" and checked in "Admin check content draft".
* This story focuses on approval decision UI/logic, status transitions, notifications, and decision history.

Acceptance criteria:

1. From the draft detail screen, admin can choose "Approve" or "Reject" for the latest draft version.
2. When rejecting, entering a comment (reason) is mandatory.
3. On approval:

* Draft status becomes "Approved".
* Draft is flagged as "Ready to post".
* KOL receives a notification with the approval status.
4. On rejection:
* Draft status becomes "Rejected – needs revision".
* KOL receives a notification including status and rejection comment.
5. System stores a full decision history per draft version:
* Decision (Approve / Reject)
* Comment
* Approver
* Timestamp
6. Once a draft version is approved:
* That version is no longer editable by the KOL.
* Any new changes require a new version (handled by the submit/resubmit story).
7. Admin can see which drafts are pending decision, e.g., via a filter "Pending approval".

column1: [CCID] Admin check content draft
column29:
Context:

* Source: CCID Current Workflow whiteboard: [https://c-channel.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/LS/whiteboard/2862710803|https://c-channel.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/LS/whiteboard/2862710803]
* Node ID in whiteboard: 6ZhB_FaAzREGHSFzpgk-3 ("1. Check content draft")
* Sits in the new "Content Draft Approval System" flow after applicants are chosen and before approve/reject.

User story:

As an admin (CS), I want to see all submitted drafts in one place with key campaign and KOL information so that I can quickly check whether each draft follows the campaign guidelines.

Details:

* Admin = CS or internal operator responsible for managing KOL drafts for a campaign.
* Drafts originate from KOLs via "submit content draft" step.
* This story focuses on the review/check step before approval or rejection.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Admin can open a "Draft list" per campaign that displays, at minimum: KOL name, channel, submission timestamp, and current status (Submitted / Resubmitted / Approved / Rejected).
2. Admin can filter or sort drafts by status and submission timestamp.
3. Admin can open a draft detail page showing: caption/text, attached creatives, hashtags, notes from KOL.
4. Admin can see previous versions of a draft (v1, v2, etc.) and the comments/decisions associated with each version.
5. From the draft detail, admin can mark a draft as "Checked" (ready for approval decision) or move directly to "Approve" or "Reject" (handled by a separate story).
6. All check actions (including who checked and when) are logged and visible in the history.

Additional notes:

* Link this story logically in the description to related stories: "KOL submit / resubmit content draft" and "Admin approve/reject content draft" once they exist in LS.

column1: [CCID] KOL can submit / resubmit content draft
column29:
User story: As a KOL, I want to submit and resubmit my content draft through a simple online form so that I can get feedback and approval without using email or spreadsheets.

Key scenarios: first-time submission, resubmission after rejection.

Acceptance criteria:

* KOL can upload or paste draft content and required assets (image, caption, hashtags, etc.).
* System associates each draft with KOL ID, campaign ID, and version (v1, v2...).
* KOL can clearly see whether this is a new submission or a resubmission.
* After submit/resubmit, KOL sees a confirmation and the draft status becomes "Submitted" or "Resubmitted".
* Admin view shows latest draft per KOL and a history of past versions.

column1: [Chat] Intermittent missing SSE messages due to in-memory EventEmitter in multi-pod setup
column29:
Currently, clients connected to our Server-Sent Events (SSE) endpoint are experiencing intermittently dropped or missing messages.

*Root Cause*

The application is currently utilizing an in-memory {{EventEmitter}} to broadcast events to connected SSE clients. Because the service is deployed on Kubernetes with multiple replicas (currently 2 pods), the SSE connections are load-balanced across the pods.

If a client's SSE connection is established with *Pod A*, but the background job or trigger that fires the event happens on *Pod B*, the in-memory {{EventEmitter}} on Pod B will fire, but Pod A will never know about it. Consequently, the client misses the message.

h3. *Proposed Solution*

Replace the local in-memory {{EventEmitter}} with a distributed Pub/Sub architecture using *Redis*.

# When an event needs to be sent, publish it to a Redis Pub/Sub channel instead of the local event emitter.
# Have all pod instances subscribe to this Redis channel.
# When a pod receives a message from the Redis subscription, it checks its local active SSE connections and forwards the message to the appropriate connected clients.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* The in-memory {{EventEmitter}} used for the SSE endpoint is completely removed/replaced.
* Redis Pub/Sub is implemented for SSE event broadcasting.
* A client connected to Pod A successfully receives events that are triggered on Pod B.
* Unit/Integration tests are updated to mock/test the new Redis Pub/Sub behavior.
* Load/manual testing in a multi-pod staging environment verifies that no messages are lost.

h3. *Technical Notes / Hints*

* Ensure Redis connection handling (reconnect strategies, error handling) is implemented properly so the SSE stream doesn't fail silently if Redis momentarily drops.
* Keep memory leaks in mind: ensure that when an SSE client disconnects, the corresponding listener/subscription logic is properly cleaned up.

column1: [CCID] CMS Manual Insights - Feedback Implementations & Upload Error Fix
column29:
We received feedback from Vilda regarding the CMS > Manual Insights form. There are a few UI/UX improvements needed, as well as a bug fix for the image upload feature.


Please address the following three items in this ticket:


Add "Reach" Field (End-to-End): Add a new input field for "Instagram reach number" in the form. (Note: The user highlighted a space next to "Saves" in the screenshot where this could go). Because this is newly tracked data, this requires a database migration, updating the backend models/output types, and adding the field to the data export function.
Fix Image Upload Bug: Users are encountering an Unsupported task type error (red toast notification) when attempting to upload photo proof in Manual Insights.
Clarify Upload Guidance: The current "Insight and Posting Proof" label is causing confusion. We need to provide more detailed guidance/instructions on what specific images are expected for Insight Proof versus Posting Proof.
Acceptance Criteria:
[ ] A database migration script is created and executed to add a new reach column (integer/numeric) to the relevant Manual Insights table.
[ ] Backend models, DTOs, and API responses (output types) are updated to accept, process, and return the new reach parameter.
[ ] The CMS Export functionality (e.g., CSV/Excel download) is updated to include "Reach" as a new column.
[ ] A numeric input field labeled "Reach" is added to the Manual Insights form and successfully saves to the database.
[ ] The Unsupported task type error during image upload is investigated and resolved. Users must be able to upload images without encountering this error.
[ ] Descriptive text or a tooltip is added near the "Upload proof images" section to clearly explain the difference and requirements for "Insight Proof" and "Posting Proof" images.

column1: [CCID] Implement 'Free Form' Questionnaire in CMS for Campaigns
column29:
*Description*

The system needs the capability to add a customizable, 'free form' questionnaire for each campaign within the CMS. This is required because when inviting KOLs to the CMS, additional information is often needed from them that is not part of the standard application.

*Details of the Requirement:*

* *Goal:* To collect campaign-specific, non-standard data from participants.
* *Examples of Data to Collect:* Kids' age, availability dates to visit a store/event, niche interests, or other custom details.
* *Reference:* The feature should be similar in functionality to the existing Lemon questionnaire.

*User Acceptance Criteria*

* *AC1:* A campaign manager must be able to create and attach a custom, 'free form' questionnaire to a campaign in the CMS.
* *AC2:* The questionnaire fields must support various input types (e.g., text, date, numerical).
* *AC3:* Campaign participants (KOLs) must be able to fill out and submit the custom questionnaire during the application or invitation process.
* *AC4:* Submitted questionnaire data must be viewable and exportable by the admin/campaign manager.

*Notes*

* The implementation should accommodate scenarios where different campaigns require entirely different sets of questions.
* The term "free form" suggests flexibility in question creation and data structure.

column1: [UI Improvement] Improve Campaign Management page for user to track the progress of each campaign
column29:
h2. *User Story:*

As a Project Management Team member, I want to view a centralized dashboard that displays all campaign progress across projects, so that I can monitor daily status and share real-time updates with clients without navigating through individual campaign pages.

Refer to CCID Project Central Sheet: [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1v2kM5zVj2JGV0fMg7llx5xRbyHIkqwYmGZ5mUvfI5KE/edit?gid=624302686#gid=624302686|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1v2kM5zVj2JGV0fMg7llx5xRbyHIkqwYmGZ5mUvfI5KE/edit?gid=624302686#gid=624302686|smart-link] 

[https://claude.ai/artifacts/252390ad-c6bc-4cba-acf1-05c05c5d1d07|https://claude.ai/artifacts/252390ad-c6bc-4cba-acf1-05c05c5d1d07|smart-link] 

*Sample protoype:*

!screencapture-claude-ai-artifacts-252390ad-c6bc-4cba-acf1-05c05c5d1d07-2026-02-04-12_01_07.png|width=667,alt="screencapture-claude-ai-artifacts-252390ad-c6bc-4cba-acf1-05c05c5d1d07-2026-02-04-12_01_07.png"!

----

h2. *Steps:*

# *Create project-level overview section*
#* Show project metadata (ID, client, period)
#* Display aggregate metrics (total campaigns, budget spent, overall timeline)
# *Build campaign progress grid view*
#* Create card-based layout for each campaign
#* Include campaign basic info (ID, name, status, phase)
#* Display timeline progress bar with percentage
#* Show KOL metrics section within each card
# *Implement KOL tracking metrics per campaign*
#* Participants progress (actual/target with percentage)
#* Winners selection status (selected/target with percentage)
#* Post submission tracking (submitted/total with percentage)
#* Visual indicators for at-risk metrics (warning icons)
# *Add filtering and sorting capabilities*
#* Status filter (All, Running, Finished, Delayed)
#* Date range filter
#* Project filter
#* Sort by timeline, status, or attention needed

----

h2. *Acceptance Criteria:*

* Dashboard displays all campaigns in card/grid format with key metrics visible at a glance
* Each campaign card shows: campaign ID, name, status, current phase, timeline progress bar
* KOL metrics are clearly displayed: participants (X/Y), winners (X/Y), posts submitted (X/Y with %)
* Progress bars visually indicate completion percentage for timeline and KOL activities
* Warning indicators appear when metrics are delayed (e.g., 0% post submission when phase is "Posting")
* "View Details" action reveals phase-by-phase breakdown and report status
* Dashboard is responsive and loads within 2 seconds with up to 50 campaigns
* UI follows existing Shadcn/UI design system (slate colors, proper spacing, shadows)

column1: [Chat] Search by Name/Number & Advanced Filters
column29:
*Summary*
[WhatsApp] Enable search by customer name/phone number and advanced filters for WhatsApp tickets

*Epic*
LS-5076

*Objective / Purpose*
Allow agents to quickly find relevant WhatsApp inquiries by searching on customer identifiers (name, phone number) and applying advanced filters (status, assignee, date range, unread, etc.). This improves efficiency and manageability as ticket volume grows.

*Steps / Subtasks*

# *Indexing & Data Model*
#* Ensure that for each WhatsApp ticket we store:
#** Customer name (if available).
#** Customer phone number (normalized format).
#* Implement or update search indexes to support:
#** Partial matches on name.
#** Exact and partial matches on phone number.
#* Confirm handling of international formats and normalization rules (e.g., E.164).
# *Search API*
#* Implement search API endpoints that support:
#** Query by name (case-insensitive, partial).
#** Query by phone number (exact and possibly partial).
#* Combine with filters:
#** Channel = WhatsApp.
#** Ticket status (open, closed, etc.).
#** Assignee (specific agent, unassigned, my tickets).
#** Date range (created or last updated).
#** Has unread messages (boolean).
# *Ticket List UI: Search & Filters*
#* Add a search bar in the WhatsApp ticket list:
#** Placeholder: e.g., “Search by name or number”.
#* Add filter controls for:
#** Channel (pre-filtered to WhatsApp or filterable).
#** Status.
#** Assignee (including “Unassigned”, “My tickets”).
#** Date range.
#** Unread only.
#* Ensure selected filters and search terms are visible and easily clearable.
# *Search Results & Performance*
#* Display results in the ticket list, including columns:
#** Customer name.
#** Phone number.
#** Status.
#** Assignee.
#** Last updated time.
#* Ensure performance is acceptable for typical volumes (define baseline if needed).
# *Validation & UX Details*
#* Normalize user input for phone numbers (strip spaces, dashes, etc.).
#* Support quick filtering patterns (e.g., typing a phone number directly in the search bar).
#* Show helpful empty-state and error messages if no results or invalid query.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* In the WhatsApp ticket list, an agent can enter a customer’s name (full or partial) in the search bar and:
** Tickets with matching customer names are returned.
** Matching is case-insensitive.
* An agent can enter a phone number (with or without formatting characters) and:
** Tickets with that customer phone number are returned.
** Normalization is applied so “+81 90-1234-5678” and “819012345678” can still match the same record (as defined by normalization rules).
* Filters work in combination with search text, e.g.:
** Search by name = “Tanaka” AND status = “Open” AND assignee = “My tickets” returns only open tickets for that agent with matching names.
* “Unread only” filter:
** When enabled, shows only WhatsApp tickets with unread messages for the current user.
* Clearing the search bar and filters resets the list to default view (e.g., all open WhatsApp tickets).
* Search results load within an acceptable time (e.g., under 2 seconds for typical dataset).
* Automated tests cover:
** Name search, number search.
** Normalization behavior.
** Combination of filters and search queries.

*Notes / Dependencies*

* Depends on accurate storage of customer name and phone number in a consistent, normalized format.
* Needs coordination with any existing global search implementation to avoid conflicting behaviors.
* Internationalization and localization of phone formatting should be considered but can be minimally supported for MVP.

column1: [Chat] Failed Message Error Handling & Retries
column29:
*Summary*
[WhatsApp] Implement robust failed message error handling and retry mechanisms

*Epic*
LS-5076

*Objective / Purpose*
Ensure that when outbound WhatsApp messages (text or media) fail, agents receive clear feedback and can retry as appropriate. This improves reliability, transparency, and trust in the system.

*Steps / Subtasks*

# *Error Classification & Mapping*
#* Identify possible error types from WhatsApp provider API:
#** Temporary errors (e.g., network, rate limit).
#** Permanent errors (e.g., invalid number, blocked by user).
#* Map provider error codes to internal error categories and human-readable messages.
# *Backend Error Handling*
#* For outbound send operations:
#** Capture and store send status (e.g., pending, sent, failed).
#** Attach error details for failed messages (type, code, message).
#* Distinguish between transient and permanent failures for retry logic.
# *Retry Strategy*
#* Define automatic retry policy for transient errors:
#** Max retry count.
#** Backoff strategy (e.g., exponential).
#* Implement manual retry endpoint:
#** Allow agent-initiated retry of a failed message if allowed by provider rules and session state.
# *Ticket UI: Error States*
#* In the conversation history:
#** Show a clear visual state for failed messages (e.g., red icon, “Failed to send” label).
#** Provide tooltip or detail on hover/click with error description.
#* For retryable failures:
#** Show a “Retry” action/button next to the failed message.
#* For non-retryable failures:
#** Indicate that the message cannot be resent and suggest alternative actions if applicable.
# *Session & Policy Constraints*
#* Ensure retry behavior respects 24-hour session rules:
#** Do not allow retry if session has expired, unless future template handling is in place.
#* If retry is blocked due to policy (e.g., session expired), inform the agent clearly.
# *Logging & Monitoring*
#* Log all send attempts, failures, and retries with:
#** Message ID, ticket ID.
#** Error details.
#** Actor (automatic vs manual retry).
#* Add metrics for failure rates and retry success.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* When an outbound message fails due to a temporary error:
** The message is marked as “Failed” in the conversation UI.
** An appropriate error message is shown to the agent (e.g., “Temporary network issue. We will retry shortly.” or similar).
** Automatic retry attempts occur according to the configured strategy.
** If an automatic retry eventually succeeds, the message status updates to “Sent” and failure indicators are cleared.
* When an outbound message fails due to a permanent error:
** The message is marked as “Failed”.
** The agent can see a reason (e.g., “The recipient’s number is invalid or blocked.”).
** No automatic retries are performed.
* For retryable failures, the agent can click “Retry”:
** A new send attempt is made.
** The outcome (success or failure) is reflected in the UI.
* If the agent tries to retry a failed message after the 24-hour window has expired:
** The retry is blocked.
** A clear message explains that the WhatsApp session has expired and the message cannot be resent.
* All failure and retry events are logged, and basic monitoring dashboards/metrics can show error rates.
* Automated tests cover:
** Error classification.
** Automatic retry behavior.
** Manual retry and UI states.

*Notes / Dependencies*

* Depends on WhatsApp provider’s error codes and limitations.
* Must align with the 24-hour session logic implemented in the session timer story.
* Design should anticipate future integration with template messages for out-of-session communication.

column1: Implement Feature Flag System (@packages/features)
column29:
h3. *🎯 Objective / Purpose*

Create a centralized feature flag system to safely merge and test new features in production without exposing them to users. This enables:

* Phased rollouts - Enable features via environment variables without code deployments

* Kill switches - Quickly disable problematic features without rolling back code

* Cross-app coordination - Toggle features across both the dashboard and API simultaneously

* Safe alpha testing - Merge incomplete features early while keeping them hidden

h3. *📋 Steps / Subtasks*

# Finalize feature flag schema and type definitions in packages/features/src/flags.ts

# Complete server-side utilities (isFeatureEnabled, getEnabledFeatures) in packages/features/src/server.ts

# Integrate featureFlagRouter into the main oRPC router (apps/api/src/routers/feature-flags/)

# Document environment variable configuration in packages/env/src/api.ts

# Create dashboard client utilities for consuming flags via oRPC

# Add comprehensive test coverage

# Write usage documentation in package README

h3. ✅ Acceptance Criteria

* Package @lemon-cms/features builds successfully (pnpm build)

* Server-side utilities work correctly (checks environment variables)

* oRPC router exposes list, get, and check procedures

* At least one demo feature flag is registered (e.g., customerSupport)

* Environment variable schema is defined in @lemon-cms/env

* Documentation includes examples for both API and dashboard usage

* Package is importable in both server and client code

column332:
2026年01月22日 11:13;712020:00bdf944-02ff-450d-a069-9ccbbf41bd5d;Issue Summary & Lesson Learned
### What Happened

* CronJob generate-influencer-import failed with ImagePullBackOff
* Image api-production was missing from ECR


### Root Cause

* ECR lifecycle policy only kept last 30 images
* The api-production tag fell out of the 30 most recent images
* CronJob config used api-production tag which was garbage collected


### What Was Fixed

# Rebuilt & pushed api-production image via GitHub Actions
# Updated ECR lifecycle policy - now only expires untagged images after 14 days
# All tagged images (*-production, *-v*, *-latest, SHA) are protected forever


### Lesson Learned

* Don't use mutable tags for production like :production or :latest
* These tags can be overwritten/deleted accidentally
* Use immutable commit-SHA tags (:api-${{ github.sha }}) for deployments
* ECR doesn't support suffix/pattern matching for tag protection
* When in doubt, protect all tagged images, only expire untagged


### Prevention

* GitHub Actions workflow deploys using SHA tags (immutable)
* Only untagged images are expired after 14 days
* Production tags are now safe from garbage collection

column1: [Chat] Real-time Inbound Message Notifications
column29:
*Objective / Purpose*

Notify agents in real time when new inbound WhatsApp messages arrive so they can respond quickly and avoid missing conversations.

----

*Steps / Subtasks (suggested)*

# *Event & Notification Model*
#* Define an internal event payload for inbound WhatsApp messages:
#** Conversation ID
#** KOL name or phone
#** Last message snippet
#** Assigned agent (if any)
#* Decide targeting: which agents should receive the notification (e.g., assigned agent only, or watchers).
# *Backend – Publish Notification Events*
#* On inbound WhatsApp message ingestion:
#** Emit a real-time event (e.g., via WebSocket / pub-sub mechanism).
#* Ensure:
#** No duplicate events for the same underlying message.
#** Events carry enough data to identify the conversation and show preview text.
# *Front-end – Real-time Subscription*
#* Subscribe to inbound message events for the current agent.
#* Show an in-app notification (e.g., toast/banner/notification center item) containing:
#** KOL name or phone number.
#** Short snippet of the latest message.
#* Clicking the notification opens the relevant conversation in the chat UI.
# *Unread / Badge Count*
#* Implement or reuse an unread-count mechanism:
#** Increment badge counts when new messages arrive and are not yet viewed.
#** Decrement/reset when the conversation is opened/read.
#* Display:
#** A badge on the chat module and/or conversation list.
# *Deduplication & Reliability*
#* Ensure that:
#** A single inbound message triggers only one notification per relevant agent.
#** Agents do not miss messages even if they open/close the chat screen.
#* Handle reconnect scenarios (e.g., re-subscription after network drop).
# *QA & Scenarios*
#* Test:
#** Single agent assigned.
#** Multiple agents viewing the same conversation (if supported).
#** Browser focus vs background tab.
#** Rapid bursts of messages (notifications remain usable, not overwhelming).

----

*Acceptance Criteria*

* When a new inbound WhatsApp message arrives:
** The relevant agent(s) receive an in-app notification.
* Notification content includes:
** KOL name (if available) or phone number.
** A snippet of the message content.
* Clicking a notification:
** Opens the correct conversation in the chat UI.
* An unread/badge count:
** Increments when new messages arrive.
** Decrements/clears when the conversation is opened/read.
* No duplicate notifications are generated for a single inbound message per agent.
* In basic end-to-end tests, no inbound message fails to trigger either:
** A notification, or
** A badge increment.
* No regression on existing chat navigation and read/unread behavior.

----

*Notes / Dependencies*

* Likely depends on:
** WebSocket or other real-time messaging infrastructure.
** Existing unread tracking (if already present).
* Future extension:
** Browser notifications or desktop push (not required for this story).
* Source context:
https://c-channel.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/~5570583a7d9a2ed3214b2a80042783dc21035c/pages/2811002882/Ticket+MVP+2+WhatsApp

column1: [Chat] 24-Hour Session Timer & UI Indicators
column29:
Objective / Purpose

Expose the WhatsApp 24-hour customer care session status per conversation so agents:

* Know whether they can send free-text messages or must use templates, and
* Avoid sending messages that violate WhatsApp policy.

Steps / Subtasks

# Session Logic & Calculation

* Implement logic to determine the start and end of the 24-hour session window based on the latest inbound (KOL) message timestamp.
* Encapsulate this in a reusable function/service that can be called per conversation.

# Backend Exposure

* Extend conversation API to return current session status (e.g., IN_WINDOW, CLOSE_TO_EXPIRY, EXPIRED) and remaining time.
* Ensure recalculation/update on new inbound messages.

# UI – Indicators in Chat Detail

* Display session status near the composer or header with remaining time and state (free-text allowed vs template-only).
* Show a warning state when remaining time is below a defined threshold (e.g., < 1 hour).

# UI – Behavior of Message Composer

* When session is inside 24h: allow free-text sending.
* When session is expired: block free-text or show a clear error explaining templates are required.
* Ensure template sending is still allowed when expired (if applicable).

# Real-time Updates

* Ensure session state and remaining time update when new inbound messages arrive.
* Refresh UI appropriately without requiring full page reload.

# QA & Edge Cases

* Test just-opened conversations with existing messages, conversations with no inbound messages, and boundary times (just before/after 24h).
* Confirm alignment with official WhatsApp Business API rules.

Acceptance Criteria

* For every conversation, the system can derive remaining time in the 24-hour window and whether it is in free-text allowed or template-only state.
* UI clearly shows remaining time, state (free-text allowed vs template-only), and a visible warning when close to expiry.
* When the window has expired, free-text messages are either blocked or show a clear error message stating templates must be used.
* Session status updates correctly after each new inbound (KOL) message.
* Logic is consistent with WhatsApp Business API documentation for 24-hour sessions.
* No regression to existing send-message and template behaviors.

Notes / Dependencies

* Relies on accurate timestamping of inbound messages from WhatsApp.
* Depends on existing send-message path for WhatsApp conversations.
* Source context: https://c-channel.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/~5570583a7d9a2ed3214b2a80042783dc21035c/pages/2811002882/Ticket+MVP+2+WhatsApp

column1: [Chat] Inquiry Assignment & Agent Ownership (PIC)
column29:
*Summary*
[Chat] Inquiry Assignment & Agent Ownership (PIC)

----

h3. Objective / Purpose

Introduce a clear “Assigned Agent (PIC)” concept for each WhatsApp chat conversation so that:

* Ownership is explicit,
* Agents avoid duplicated responses, and
* Conversations can later be filtered/reported by assignee.

----

h3. Steps / Subtasks (suggested)

You can split these into actual Jira subtasks:

# *Data Model & API*
#* Add an {{assignedAgent}} (PIC) field to the conversation entity/model.
#* Update/create APIs to:
#** Get the assigned agent per conversation.
#** Set/change the assigned agent (with basic validation).
# *Assignment Behavior & Rules*
#* Define default behavior:
#** New conversations start as “Unassigned” unless there is an existing rule.
#* Define who can:
#** Assign an unassigned conversation.
#** Reassign an existing conversation to another agent (e.g., all agents vs. supervisors only).
# *UI – Chat Detail View*
#* Display current assigned agent in the chat detail (e.g., header or sidebar).
#* Show “Unassigned” if no PIC exists.
#* Provide a control (dropdown, button) to:
#** Assign an agent.
#** Reassign to a different agent.
#* Ensure updates are reflected immediately after assignment.
# *UI – Chat List View*
#* Show assigned agent for each conversation in the list (e.g., avatar, name, or column).
#* Indicate “Unassigned” conversations clearly.
#* Ensure the information is readable at list scale (e.g., truncation, tooltip).
# *Auditing / History (Minimal)*
#* At minimum, log who changed the assignment and when for debugging.
#* If easily supported, store assignment history (optional, based on scope).
# *Permissions & Security*
#* Respect existing user roles and permissions.
#* Restrict assignment actions to permitted roles only.
# *QA & Validation*
#* Test:
#** Assigning and reassigning conversations.
#** Visibility in both list and detail views.
#** Unassigned state behavior.
#** Permission edge cases (e.g., non-privileged user trying to reassign).

----

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* Each conversation has an {{Assigned Agent (PIC)}} field in the data model.
* In the chat detail view:
** The assigned agent (name or display name) is clearly displayed.
** “Unassigned” is visibly shown if no PIC is set.
** A user with appropriate permissions can assign and reassign the PIC via the UI.
* In the chat list view:
** The assigned agent is visible for each conversation (or “Unassigned” where applicable).
** Changes to assignment are reflected without page reload or with clear refresh behavior.
* Assignment/reassignment actions are restricted to permitted roles only.
* Basic logging exists for assignment changes (who changed, when).
* Feature works for existing conversations as well as new ones.
* No regression on existing chat functionality.

----

h3. Notes / Dependencies

* Intended to work together with:
** Advanced Filters (Assignee filter) – Ticket 7.
* Must integrate with existing user/agent identity system.
* Source context:
https://c-channel.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/~5570583a7d9a2ed3214b2a80042783dc21035c/pages/2811002882/Ticket+MVP+2+WhatsApp

As a *KOL Team member*, I want to *have a consistent export format for participant data that spans columns A to N*, so that *I can easily copy and paste the data into other sheets or tools without needing to manually re-align or clean up columns*.


*💡 Background / Why it matters*
The current export format for participant data is not consistently aligned with the team's internal standard (Columns A to N), which causes friction. This inconsistency requires extra manual work for data management and transfer, introducing potential human error.

* *Given* I initiate an export of participant data, *when* the export file is generated, *then* the columns must consistently range from A to N, and the column order must match the standard defined in the reference document.
* *Given* I have the exported data, *when* I copy a column (e.g., column B), *then* it should contain the intended data point (e.g., KOL Name) as per the A to N format specification.

column1: Refactor Fat API into modular Packages with proper documentation and tests
column29:
h1. [Refactor] Decouple Monolithic apps/api into Modular Packages

h3. 🎯 Objective / Purpose

The current apps/api is a "fat" application containing mixed concerns (DB, Email, RPC, Jobs, SDK). This tight coupling makes the codebase difficult to maintain, slows down testing, and complicates scaling.

The goal of this task is to split these concerns into independent, modular packages to improve code reusability, enforce boundaries, and streamline our CI/CD pipelines.

h3. 📋 Steps / Subtasks

# *Architecture Setup:* Define the new package structure (e.g., packages/db, packages/email, packages/sdk, etc.).
# *Code Migration:* Extract logic from apps/api into their respective packages.
# *Documentation & Quality:* Add a comprehensive README.md and unit tests for every newly created package.
# *Refactor Imports:* Update all internal imports/exports within apps/* to point to the new package locations.
# *DevOps Updates:*
#* Modify Dockerfile to account for the new workspace structure and build layers.
#* Update GitHub Actions workflows to support multi-package testing and deployment.

h3. ✅ Acceptance Criteria

* apps/api logic is successfully decoupled into standalone packages.
* All new packages include a README.md explaining usage and a test suite with passing coverage.
* The main application (apps/*) functions correctly using the new package imports.
* Docker images build successfully and the application runs in a containerized environment.
* GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines pass (linting, testing, and building).
* No regression in existing features (Email, RPC, Jobs, etc.).

h3. 🧪 Notes / Dependencies

* *Monorepo Tooling:* This task assumes we are using a workspace manager (e.g., Turborepo, and pnpm workspaces).
* *Circular Dependencies:* Take extra care to ensure that the new packages do not have circular dependencies during the split.
* *Build Performance:* Monitor if this change affects the total build time in GitHub Actions.

column1: [UI/UX Improvement] Persist checkbox selection for KOLs and show final confirmation list
column29:
h3. *📌 1. Overview*

When selecting multiple KOLs via checkboxes, the selection is not consistently preserved, and users cannot clearly confirm the final list of selected KOLs before proceeding.

This ticket aims to improve selection persistence and add a final confirmation step.

This applies for both participants and winners list page.

E.g. [https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/12/participants|https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/12/participants|smart-link] 

[https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/12/winner|https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/12/winner|smart-link] 

----

h3. *🧩 2. Background / Problem*

* Users select multiple KOLs using checkboxes across a list or table
* Current issues:
** Checkbox selections are *lost* when:
*** Navigating between pages
*** Applying filters / sorting
*** Returning from another screen
** Users *cannot clearly see* which KOLs are finally selected before the next action (e.g. invite / send / proceed)

This causes:

* User confusion
* Risk of sending actions to the wrong KOLs
* Repeated re-selection work

----

h3. *👤 3. User / Role*

* Internal operator / KOL Team

----

h3. *🎯 4. User Goal*

As a admin user, I want my selected KOLs to remain checked and to clearly confirm the final list before proceeding, so I can confidently perform the next action without mistakes.

----

h3. *🧪 5. Requirements / Expected Behavior*

h4. *5.1 Checkbox persistence*

* Selected KOL checkboxes should remain *checked* when:

** Paginating the list
** Applying or removing filters
** Sorting columns
** Navigating back from another screen
* 
* Selection state should be managed on the *frontend state* (or backend if required by design)



h4. *5.2 Final confirmation view*

* Before executing the final action (e.g. send invite / assign / proceed):

** Show a *confirmation screen or modal*
** Display:

*** Total number of selected KOLs
*** A list (or summary) of selected KOLs (e.g. name + ID)
** 
* User can:

** Confirm and proceed
** Go back and adjust the selection

----

h3. *✅ 6. Acceptance Criteria*

* Selected KOLs remain checked across pagination, filtering, and sorting
* Selected state is not lost when navigating between screens
* A final confirmation step is displayed before execution
* Confirmation view correctly reflects the selected KOL list
* User can cancel and return to selection without losing state

----

h3. *⚠️ 7. Notes / Edge Cases*

* Consider max selection size (performance impact if many KOLs are selected)
* UX decision needed:

** Modal vs dedicated confirmation page
* 
* Confirm expected behavior when:

** KOL becomes unavailable during selection
** Filter removes a previously selected KOL from view
*

column1: Add “ID” and "Company Name" Column For KOL Agency in Participant Import Template
column29:
h3. 🎯 Objective / Purpose
Why this task is important. What problem it solves or what value it brings.

As an Admin user, I want to *include the Agency ID in the participant import template*, so that I can assign participants to their respective agencies in one upload without repeating the import process.

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, when importing participants via CSV, there’s no way to associate them with different KOL agencies in one go. Admins must upload multiple files per agency, which is time-consuming and error-prone.

Adding an “ ID” , “Company Name” column will allow bulk assignment in a single upload and streamline campaign setup.

[https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/5/import|https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/5/import|smart-link] 

h3. ✅ Acceptance Criteria
(What must be true for this task to be considered done)

* Remove select company in import page
* Show Company ID Column in Company page [https://cms.lemon.co.id/admin/companies|https://cms.lemon.co.id/admin/companies|smart-link] 
* Template to add Company ID column to map KOL agency for each participant



* *Given* I prepare a participant import CSV
* *When* I include a column “Agency ID” with valid values
* *Then* each participant is correctly assigned to their respective agency upon import
* If “Agency ID” is blank, participant is treated as unassigned
* System validates Agency ID (must match existing agency records)



h3. 🧪 Notes / Dependencies
(Optional: Include blocked tasks, other tickets, or implementation notes)

* Does not auto-create new agencies

column1: Add KOL Profile URL & Sort by Import Date in Exported Participant Excel File
column29:
h3. 🎯 Objective / Purpose
Why this task is important. What problem it solves or what value it brings.

As an Admin user, I want to *see each participant’s KOL profile URL* and have the exported data *sorted by the import date in ascending order*, so that I can review the participants more easily and share complete, organized data externally.

h3. 💡 Background / Why it matters

Currently, the exported participant Excel file lacks direct access to the KOL profile URL, which is often required for campaign reporting or communication.
Additionally, the data is not sorted, which causes confusion when tracking the order of KOL import or filtering newly added participants.
This enhancement will improve data usability and reduce manual effort in post-export cleanup.

h3. ✅ Acceptance Criteria
(What must be true for this task to be considered done)

* *Given* I export the participants of a campaign
* *When* the file is downloaded
* *Then* it includes a new column labeled “Profile URL”
* *And* all rows are sorted in ascending order based on the “Import Date”
* KOL Profile URL format: e.g., [https://www.instagram.com/{username}|https://www.instagram.com/{username}]

column1: Fix ENG Formula for TikTok Gifting report
column29:
h3. *Description*

The individual engagement rate formula used in TikTok gifting report is incorrect.

h3. *Current (Wrong) Formula*

{noformat}followers / (views + likes + comments + shares){noformat}

h3. *Correct Formula (Required)*

{noformat}views / (likes + comments + shares){noformat}

Reference sheet with correct calculation:

column1: Fix ENG Rate Formula & Update Report Output Rules for Gifting (Instagram/X/TikTok)
column29:
h3. *Description*

Several issues were found in the gifting Excel reports exported from CMS:

# *ENG Rate (Average Engagement Rate) in cell I3 is incorrect*
Average ER Formula, and show in percentage

{noformat}=AVERAGE(J7:J103){noformat}

Correct Formula ER:

{noformat}=IFERROR((G7+H7+I7)/E7,"-"){noformat}

# *ENG Rate should be displayed as a percentage format*
# *External IG Report: Remove Post “Views” value*
#* Reels views should remain
#* Internal report keeps all views
# 
# *For comments count:*
#* If the insight is _not_ linked, display "–"
#* Currently, comments show "0" while “saves” correctly show "–"

Reference correct spreadsheet:

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1wSjClgv2KM_5kSp4swMh3AUSYM4lAlLD/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=114796825609974043486&rtpof=true&sd=true|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1wSjClgv2KM_5kSp4swMh3AUSYM4lAlLD/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=114796825609974043486&rtpof=true&sd=true|smart-link] 

Reference current incorrect CMS report:

[^cp3816_external_report (1).xlsx]

h3. *Expected Behavior / Requirements*

h4. *1. Fix Average ENG Rate (I3)*

* Calculate using correct formula (confirm business logic if needed)
* Result must be formatted as *percentage (e.g., 3.25%)*

h4. *2. External IG Report – Impressions logic*

* *Feed post impressions → remove (blank)*
* *Reels impressions → keep*
* Internal report → no change (keep both feed + reels impressions)

h4. *3. Display for Comments in all gifting reports*

* If insight data for comments is *missing*, display "–"
* If insight exists and value is 0 → show "0" (normal)

h4. *4. Saves column*

* Behavior unchanged (already correct)

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* ENG rate now matches the logic shown in reference sheet
* ENG rate appears in percentage format
* External IG/X/TikTok report does NOT show feed impressions
* External IG/X/TikTok report still shows reels impressions
* Internal IG/X/TikTok report shows all impressions
* Missing comment insights show "–"
* Regression tested for IG/X/TT common gifting report logic

column1: Add Blacklist Flag for KOL and Sync to Campaign Participant Management
column29:
*Description:*

We need to implement a blacklist function for KOLs.

When a KOL is marked as blacklisted, the system should:

# Add a *blacklist flag* to the KOL record.
# Allow entering a *blacklist note* (reason / memo).
# Automatically *blacklist the participant* in all relevant campaigns.
# *Update the KOL database* to reflect the blacklist status.

----

*User Story:*

As an admin,

I want to mark a KOL as blacklisted with a note,

so that the system prevents them from participating in campaigns and keeps our KOL records accurate.

----

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* A “Blacklist” toggle/flag is added to the KOL profile.
* A text field allows adding a blacklist note.
* When blacklisted, the KOL is automatically marked as “blacklisted” in any campaign they joined.
* The KOL database updates immediately with the blacklist status and note.
* UI clearly displays blacklist status.
* API returns blacklist flag + note in KOL data response.
* Blacklisted KOLs cannot be selected or approved in campaigns.

column1: Add PJID Field to Campaign Creation (Required Field)
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I want to *add a PJID field when creating a campaign*, so that I can ensure every campaign is linked to a valid project reference for internal tracking and reporting.

[https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/create|https://cms.lemon.co.id/campaigns/create]

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, there’s no structured way to link a campaign to a project ID (PJID), which causes inconsistency in tracking and reporting across systems.

By making PJID a required field, we can enforce data integrity and simplify cross-functional reporting and analysis (e.g., Finance, Marketing, Client Ops).

----

✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* I am on the campaign creation page
* *When* I fill in the form
* *Then* there is a required input field labeled “PJID”
* The form should not be submitted if PJID is left blank
* PJID should be stored and visible in the campaign detail view
* PJID can be edited later (optional if included in scope)

h3. Reference

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-aKF1mN8NFizxwPczvqlZ97mQ8yuWB6d2WLXs1YMaSw/edit?gid=136825699#gid=136825699|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-aKF1mN8NFizxwPczvqlZ97mQ8yuWB6d2WLXs1YMaSw/edit?gid=136825699#gid=136825699|smart-link]

🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I want to *bulk import Instagram Reel share and save counts*, so that I can save time and generate reports more efficiently.

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, share and save metrics for Instagram Reels are either manually entered or not tracked at all. These metrics are important for evaluating influencer performance and are often requested by clients.

Adding a bulk upload function will streamline post-campaign reporting and reduce manual data entry.

[~accountid:60a217d65998a60068418a4b] please share with me the sample template when you start to work on this

* User goes to Winner tab and select import manual insight for Instagram
* *Given* I have a CSV file with KOL Reel post data including share and save counts
* *When* I upload it via the CMS bulk import feature
* *Then* the system should update each KOL post with the correct share and save data
* The file format and required fields should be clearly documented
* Import errors (e.g., missing KOL ID, invalid URLs) should be logged and shown to user

column1: Allow admin user to add participant manually in a campaign
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I want to *manually add a campaign participant (KOL)* to a campaign, so that I can include last-minute or external KOLs who are not in the original uploaded list.

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, participants are added via CSV upload, which is not flexible when a single KOL needs to be added or corrected.

Manual addition will improve campaign agility, reduce upload errors, and support smaller or ad-hoc campaign adjustments.

----

✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* I am on the campaign detail or participant list page
* *When* I click “Add Participant”
* *Then* a form appears allowing me to input:
search for KOL in db, if exist add. If new, add participant:
** Name
** Platform (Instagram, TikTok, etc.)
** Username / SNS URL
** Follower count
** Rate card
* 
* Upon saving, the KOL is added to the campaign’s participant list
* Data is saved and editable later via the participant detail page



h3. 🧪 Notes / Dependencies
(Optional: Include blocked tasks, other tickets, or implementation notes)

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-aKF1mN8NFizxwPczvqlZ97mQ8yuWB6d2WLXs1YMaSw/edit?gid=136825699#gid=136825699|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-aKF1mN8NFizxwPczvqlZ97mQ8yuWB6d2WLXs1YMaSw/edit?gid=136825699#gid=136825699|smart-link]

column1: Participant page to allow admin user to edit KOL participant data manually
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I want to *view and edit details of a campaign participant (KOL)* on a dedicated page, so that I can manage participant information easily and accurately.

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, there is no dedicated detail page for each campaign participant.

Admins often need to check or update KOL information (e.g., Instagram handle, contact info, rate card) during campaign execution or post-reporting.

Having a centralized page per participant will reduce manual back-and-forth and improve data accuracy.



✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* I click on a KOL/participant from a campaign view
* *When* the system navigates to a detail page
* *Then* I can see participant details such as:
** Name, username, platform
** Rate card, contact info
** Participated campaigns
* I can edit fields like:
** Email, phone number, platform ID, rate card
* A “Save” button should confirm updates
* Validation is triggered for required fields



h3. 🧪 Notes / Dependencies
(Optional: Include blocked tasks, other tickets, or implementation notes)

column1: Handling BrightData API request in queue
column29:
h3. 🎯 Objective / Purpose

Since there is rate limit from BrightData API (60 requests per hour, up to 10,000 URLs per request, 200 MB input)

We need to control the BrightData API request in a queue.

h3. 📋 Steps / Subtasks (if needed)

# Modify the file import API to write into the BrightData queue
# Write job to fetch from the BD queue and hit the API (max. 60 request per hour)
# Write a job to check the status of already requested API and update the data in our DB accordingly

column1: Export KOL List with Data in CSV Format
column29:
h3. 🎯 User Story

As a KOL admin user,
I want to export the KOL (influencer) list with update profile data metrics into a CSV file,
so that I can share it to client without manually copying data.

h3. 💡 Background / Why it matter

(Briefly explain why the feature is needed or the current problem)

Currently, there is no export function for KOL lists. Admin user have to manually copy data from , which is time-consuming and error-prone. Having an export-to-CSV feature will improve operational efficiency and support screening of KOL for both internal and external use.

h3. ✅ Acceptance Criteria

(Use Given/When/Then format for clear pass/fail rules)

Scenario 1: Exporting KOL participant list in a campaign

* Given I am viewing a specific campaign participant list in the CMS
* When I click the “Export List” button
* Then a CSV file is downloaded containing the KOL list data with the following columns:
** Campaign
** Platform
** Tier	
** Category	
** Gender	
** Username	
** Link	
** Followers	
** ER	
** Avg Views

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=463531186#gid=463531186|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=463531186#gid=463531186|smart-link] 

Scenario 2: Edge case — special characters

* Given KOL names or fields contain special characters (e.g., emoji, non-ASCII text)
* When the CSV is downloaded
* Then the encoding should support UTF-8 to ensure proper display

h3. 🧪 Out of Scope / Notes

(Clarify what’s not included, edge cases, or notes from stakeholders/devs)

Check the {{Export KOL Profile Metrics}} process, this task is focus on this part (diagram as below)

column1: Upload KOL List in CSV Format
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I can upload a KOL list in CSV format, so that I can save time instead of uploading one by one.

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, Admins must register KOLs individually, which is slow and inefficient when onboarding multiple influencers. Bulk upload via CSV will streamline this process, reduce manual work, and ensure data consistency by enforcing a standard template for KOL information.

----

✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* I am on the KOL Management page
*When* I click “Upload CSV” and select a file
*Then* the system should validate the file format and check required columns (e.g., name, sns_platform, sns_handle, follower_count, email).
* *Given* the CSV passes validation
*When* I confirm upload
*Then* the system should create new KOL records in bulk and display them in the KOL list.
* *Given* the CSV contains errors (e.g., missing required columns, invalid SNS platform value)
*When* I attempt to upload
*Then* the system should reject the file and provide an error summary.
* *Given* only some rows contain errors
*When* I upload
*Then* the system should allow partial success (valid rows are imported) and provide an error report CSV for failed rows.
* *Given* the upload is successful
*When* I return to the KOL list
*Then* I should see the newly added KOLs, ready for campaign assignment.

* {color:#bf2600}Duplicate detection and merge rules are handled{color}

----

🧪 *Out of Scope / Notes*

* 

----

📎 *Attachments / References*

* [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=463531186#gid=463531186|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=463531186#gid=463531186|smart-link]

column1: Export Data Report of KOL Creative Post
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I can export a cleansed data report to get the latest numbers of KOL creatives, so that I can use accurate and consolidated data for reporting.

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, Admins must manually compile influencer creative performance data from multiple sources (manual inputs, CSV uploads, API-fetched insights). This often leads to duplicate entries, inconsistent formatting, and errors in reporting. By enabling export of a *cleansed* report, the system ensures that Admins receive deduplicated, validated, and standardized data that can be shared internally or with clients.

There’s 2 main purpose to resolve: 

# Weekly report (Every Monday: Flash report to share with client)
# Final report (7 days after posting of all KOLs' post)

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1139031555#gid=1139031555|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1139031555#gid=1139031555|smart-link] 

----

✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* I am on the Campaign Report page
*When* I click “Export Report”
*Then* the system should generate a file (Excel/CSV) that consolidates the latest metrics (reach, engagement, likes, comments, shares, ER, etc.) for all KOL creatives in the campaign.
* *Given* duplicate or conflicting records exist (e.g., manual entry + API entry for the same post)
*When* I export the report
*Then* the system should apply deduplication rules (latest valid entry wins, with audit trail).
* *Given* I choose export options
*When* I export
*Then* the output should reflect my selected preferences.
* *Given* the export completes successfully
*When* I open the file
*Then* I should see standardized formatting (one row per creative post, clean column headers, no null/duplicate values).
* *Given* the export fails (e.g., due to large data volume)
*When* I attempt it
*Then* the system should notify me with an error message and suggest retry or background job export.

----

🧪 *Out of Scope / Notes*

* Advanced BI dashboards or visualizations are not included (covered by analytics integration).
* Scheduled/automatic exports (e.g., daily sync) are separate future work.
* Cross-campaign aggregated exports are out of scope for this ticket.

----

📎 *Attachments / References*

* [CMS Feature (ID_JP) – Scope CSV]
* [https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Eul7Xy3b-7ie2fI1HALUlt09QKkLsFmjNN7n9lBA3f4/edit?slide=id.g384e9f6e054_0_62&pli=1#slide=id.g384e9f6e054_0_62|https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Eul7Xy3b-7ie2fI1HALUlt09QKkLsFmjNN7n9lBA3f4/edit?slide=id.g384e9f6e054_0_62&pli=1#slide=id.g384e9f6e054_0_62|smart-link]

column1: Get Latest KOL SNS Data via Webscraper API
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I can view the latest KOL SNS profile and post insights via the WebScraper API, so that I do not need to manually check posts one by one.

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, Admins have to open each influencer’s SNS profile or request screenshots to view post performance (followers, likes, views, engagement). This is time-consuming and prone to errors. By integrating the Brightdata’s API, the CMS can fetch the latest post insights directly from Instagram/Tiktok, reducing manual effort and providing Admins with real-time, accurate performance data.

----

✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* a KOL’s Instagram account is linked to the CMS via Meta Business API permissions
*When* I open the campaign’s Post Insights page
*Then* the system should fetch and display the latest available insights (public data: likes, comments, shares, engagement rate, etc.) via API.
* *Given* multiple KOLs are assigned to the campaign
*When* I view the campaign’s insights
*Then* all linked KOL post data should be retrieved and displayed in a consolidated view.
* *Given* a KOL has multiple campaign-related posts
*When* I view their insights
*Then* the system should fetch metrics for all posts tagged or mapped to the campaign.
* *Given* the API call fails (e.g., expired token, invalid permissions)
*When* I try to fetch insights
*Then* the system should display an error message and log the failure for Admin review.
* *Given* post insights are retrieved successfully
*When* I export campaign reports
*Then* the API-fetched metrics should be included in the export file.

----

🧪 *Out of Scope / Notes*

* Historical insight fetching (beyond API limits) is out of scope.
* Data caching, sync frequency (e.g., daily/hourly), and retries will be defined in technical tasks.

----

📎 *Attachments / References*

* [CMS Feature (ID_JP) – Scope CSV]
* [Meta Business Discovery API Documentation|https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-api/reference/business-discovery]
* Related tickets: _Upload CSV Post Insight Data_, _Manual Post Insight Update_, _Upload Creative Post Screenshots_
* [https://docs.brightdata.com/api-reference/web-scraper-api/social-media-apis/tiktok|https://docs.brightdata.com/api-reference/web-scraper-api/social-media-apis/tiktok|smart-link] 
* [https://docs.brightdata.com/api-reference/web-scraper-api/social-media-apis/instagram|https://docs.brightdata.com/api-reference/web-scraper-api/social-media-apis/instagram|smart-link]

column1: Upload CSV Creative Post Insight Data
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I can upload CSV post insight data so that I can save time instead of uploading one by one.

----

💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, Admins manage post insights (likes, comments, shares, reach, etc.) manually for each influencer post in Google sheet. By allowing bulk upload of insights through a CSV file, Admins can save time, reduce manual effort, and ensure data consistency.

----

✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* I am on the Campaign Insights page
*When* I click “Upload CSV” and select a file
*Then* the system should validate the file format ({color:#bf2600}columns: TBD{color}).
[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1139031555#gid=1139031555|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1139031555#gid=1139031555|smart-link] → to check with Viola / Vilda again

* *Given* the file passes validation
*When* I confirm upload
*Then* all rows should be imported into the system and linked to the correct posts.
* *Given* the file has errors (e.g., missing required columns, invalid data types, unknown post_id)
*When* I upload it
*Then* the system should reject the file and show detailed error messages.
* *Given* partial rows have issues
*When* I upload
*Then* the system should allow me to download an error report CSV with the failed rows highlighted.
* *Given* I successfully upload data
*When* I view the campaign insights dashboard
*Then* the metrics should reflect the newly uploaded insights.

----

🧪 *Out of Scope / Notes*

* API-based auto-sync (e.g., with Meta or TikTok APIs) is separate task.

----

📎 *Attachments / References*

* [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1139031555#gid=1139031555|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1139031555#gid=1139031555|smart-link]

column1: Filter and search in Campaign
column29:
🎯 *User Story*

As an Admin user, I want to filter and search campaigns so that I can get the right result.



💡 *Background / Why it matters*

Currently, Admin users must manually scroll through long campaign lists, making it time-consuming and error-prone to locate specific campaigns. Adding robust search and filter functionality will improve usability, reduce operational effort, and allow faster access to the right campaign data. This is especially important as the number of campaigns grows.

----

✅ *Acceptance Criteria*

* *Given* I am on the Campaign List page
*When* I type a keyword (e.g., campaign name, brand name) in the search bar
*Then* I should see only the campaigns that match the keyword.
* *Given* I am on the Campaign List page
*When* I apply a filter (e.g., status = “Running”, date range = “This Month”)
*Then* the list should update to show only campaigns that meet the filter criteria.
* *Given* multiple filters are selected
*When* I apply them
*Then* only campaigns that satisfy all selected filters should appear.
* *Given* filters are applied
*When* I click “Clear Filters”
*Then* the system should reset and display the full campaign list.

----

🧪 *Out of Scope / Notes*

* Advanced analytics (e.g., sorting by performance metrics) is not part of this story.

----

📎 *Attachments / References*

* Campaign List Filter Sheet: [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1936604411#gid=1936604411|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1c-qsiBKbShJKCqHzAHghAsvLWkizp_2OnyfcoDsfJOQ/edit?gid=1936604411#gid=1936604411|smart-link]
